A blood-member of the Salviati family(to whom Pope Sixtus IV had re-awarded the papal banking contract after taking it away from the Medici), he was also related by marriage to the Pazzi, Medici,  ...    In 1464, Salviati moved to Rome to attach himself to Francesco della Rovere– who later became Pope Sixtus IV – and his nephews, Girolamo and Pietro Riario. This paid off in his appointment as archbishop. The Medici family, who ruled the Republic of Florence at the time, opposed his appointment as archbishop, and so Salviati met with several other individuals dissatisfied with the Medici's rule to plan the assassination of Lorenzo and Giuliano de'Medici, co-rulers of Florence, in what later became known as the Pazzi conspiracy.
